1,5 Ways to keep your eyes healthy,"Our eyes are among the most significant parts of our body. They are essential for our everyday living, and for our work. If you don't want to live in darkness forever, you need to take care of your eyes. Here are a few helpful things you can do to keep your eyes healthy. ","

Try to close your eyes and imagine how's life in darkness or in dim light. Do you think you can be able to properly do the things you are used to? Our eyes are among the most significant parts of our body. They are essential for our everyday living, and for our work. If you don't want to live in darkness forever, you need to take care of your eyes.
There are many things you can do to keep your eyes healthy. Among these things are as follows:

We all have known that the sun's ultraviolet rays are harmful to our skin. This is one main reason why we use lotions and creams with high SPF. But the UV rays do not only harm the skin, they also give negative effects to our eyes. Exposure to sun's UV rays has been proven to cause or contribute to the development or progress of various eye conditions like cataract, age-related macular degeneration, and short-sightedness.

column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Protective sunglasses "" alt=""Protective sunglasses "" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/index/bestglassesa_1_2_4.jpg""}}"" height=""305"" width=""458"" />

column1: Wearing a protective sunglasses can help you shield your eyes from the harmful rays of the sun. Make sure to pick high-quality sunglasses like California Glasses with 100% UV protection.

Lack of sleep can lead to various health problems. As much as possible, make it a habit to sleep at least eight hours per night.  It is the best time for your eyes to take a rest. Not only that a good sleep can help you in maintaining a healthy body, it can also help you avoid waking up with puffy, strained, and tired eyes.

Water is essential to your overall wellness. Your eyes also need to stay hydrated all the time. Drink at least eight glasses of fluid per day. Lack of fluid in the body can cause eye irritation and dryness. If you experience dryness or irritation, try to contact an eye doctor or get your eyes a hydrating eye drop.

Whether it is with your hands or used towels, don't try to rub your eyes. Your eyes are very sensitive and they can easily be infected or irritated when in contact with dirty things. If you really need to touch your eyes, sunglasses, eyeglasses, or contact lens, make sure to disinfect your hands first. Thoroughly wash your hands regularly to get rid of the dirt, bacteria, and dust.

A healthy body and lifestyle are essential to the quality of your life. If your health is poor, the quality of your life is also compromised. The overall health can likewise be linked to the health of your eyes. There are certain diseases that can cause blindness and other eye problems. Obesity, for instance, increases your risks to diabetes which is known to cause blindness.

For optimum health, make sure that you exercise regularly and maintain a healthy diet. Eat foods that contain vitamins and minerals that are essential for eye health like Beta-carotene, Lutein, Vitamin A, Vitamin C, Vitamin E, Omega-3, and Lycopene.

2,Different ways to protect your eyes from computer-related eye problems,"Whether you are using your computer for socialization, work, or entertainment, you have to make sure that you keep your eyes protected from computer vision syndrome or CVS and other computer-related eye problems. Here are a few effective ways to protect your eyes from eyestrain, computer vision syndrome, and other computer-related eye problems.","
column2: >With almost everything getting computerized nowadays, many people are using a computer for many reasons- for socialization, work, and entertainment. Regardless of the reason why you use a computer, you have to make sure that you keep your eyes protected from computer vision syndrome or CVS.
>There are different ways to protect your eyes from eyestrain, computer vision syndrome, and other computer-related eye problems. Here are some of these effective ways:
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ? Take regular short breaks
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Use computer glasses
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Exercise your eyes
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Modify computer display settings
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Get your eyes checked
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Ensure proper workstation illumination
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Observe proper distance from your computer screen
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Use larger icon and font size
>? ? ? ? ? ? ?--? ? ??Take a short break

>Even if you need to keep your focus on what you are doing, you still have to take a short break from the computer from time to time. You can stand up and do a quick body stretching at least once in every 20-30 minutes to release muscle fatigue on specific parts of your body and to reduce tension on your neck, back, legs, shoulders, fingers, and arms as well. Both your eyes and your body will thank you for doing this on a regular basis.

column3: margin-right: auto

column2: >Exercise your eyes
>Don't just stare at your computer all throughout your work hours. Try to exercise them every now and then by keeping your sight off the screen every 10-15 minutes.
>Observe proper distance from your computer screen
>The most appropriate distance between the face and the computer screen is around 20 to 30 inches. Keep this proper distance to reduce eye strain.
>Use larger icon and font size
>Keep the icon and font sizes large enough to make it easier for you to see them within the appropriate working distance.
>Modify computer display settings

>Adjust the display settings of your computer like the brightness, text contrast, and color temperature. Make sure that its brightness is just the same as the surrounding- not too bright and not too dull and dark. Also, reduce the color temperature to ensure long-term viewing comfort.

column1: 3,Why is it important to have a well-done eyeglasses prescription?,Doing your eyeglasses prescription isn?t something you take lightly. Do you know what eyeglass prescription entails? Read further to know more.,"
column2: >If you decide on making your first pair of glasses, or you just want to change your old ones, you will need an eyeglass prescription. The first step is visiting your ophthalmologist or optometrist. What many don’t know is the fact that the prescription is first written on a piece of paper, which contains your personal details required to make your own eyeglasses. Not many understand what this prescription means. In this piece, I will try to explain all you need to know about eyeglasses prescription.
>Importance of frequent eyeglasses prescription

>If you think that once you did your eyeglasses prescription, you can use it for life, it may surprise you this is not true. An ophthalmologist or eye doctors recommend carrying out a routine checkup for those using contact lenses and eyeglasses at least once every two years. Most eyeglasses prescription expires after two years.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Eyeglasses "" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Our eyes and bodies undergo constant change, which is why it is important to have a well-done eyeglasses prescription. Doing an eye check will ensure your eyeglasses or prescription lenses give you the optimal vision you require. Merely looking at the eye, it is difficult to detect any significant deterioration in your eyesight
column3: however, an eye test can easily detect these changes. Additionally, if the vision of one of the eye changes, the other may compensate for it. This makes it hard for one to become aware of any deterioration. The hard truth is that leaving your eye unchecked could cause nausea, headaches, and eyestrain.

column2: >In the course of your eye examination, the ophthalmologist may check the overall health condition of your eye. Regular checkup will help your doctor detect many eye diseases that may occur without prior signs. The doctor may also measure the intraocular pressure while testing for warning signs of glaucoma and signs of macular degeneration. If these are undetected, it could result in permanent loss of sight, which you won’t want considering the importance of vision. Nevertheless, with the advances in technology, doing your eye checkup shouldn’t be a challenge anymore.
>Reading eyeglass prescription without an ophthalmologist

>Have you gone to the ophthalmologist and done your eyeglass prescription only to be confused by the inscriptions on the paper. Well, you shouldn’t be because it is as simple as ABC if you follow the explanations below.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Reading Eyeglasses"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Looking at the eyeglasses prescription given to you by your doctor, you will notice certain numbers listed under the headings of OD and OS. These are Latin abbreviations that mean oculus dextrus (OD) for the right eye and oculus sinister (OS) for the left eye. Intermittently, another notation OU may be used
column3: it means something that involves both eyes. Generally, the farther the number is from zero on the prescription, the worse your eyesight is and the more vision correction you will need.

column2: >When you see a “minus” (-) sign in front of the number, it signifies that you are nearsighted but a “plus” (+) sign signifies farsighted. These numbers are a representation of diopters. Diopter is the unit used in meaning the correction or focusing power of your lens required for your eye. It is mostly abbreviated D.

>For instance, if your eyeglasses prescription reads a -1.00, it means you are one diopter of nearsightedness. However, if it reads -4.25, it means you have 4 and ¼ diopters of nearsightedness. This second reading is more nearsighted when compared to the first and will require a thicker or stronger lens. In the same vein, a +5 would be more when compared to +1.
>People who have issues with astigmatism, they will have three different numbers in their prescription. The general writing is as follow:
>S x C x Axis

>The S refers to the “spherical” aspect of the eyeglass prescription. This is the degree of farsightedness or nearsightedness as explained earlier. The C refers to astigmatism or “cylinder”, and could be apositive or negative number. The more the number, the more astigmatism you have. A primary cause of astigmatism is cornea, which has the shape of a football. The Axis refers to anumber between 0 and 180 degrees. It shows the orientation of astigmatism.
>How is Prescription eyeglasses made?

>Eyeglasses are important for people having vision issues. Their invention dated back to centuries ago and has brought improvement in people’s vision. Wye glasses enable people to do what they couldn’t do before such as reading a book or driving a car. The human lens and eyeglasses utilize the same principle and work by supplementing the lens function.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Eyeglasses Prescription"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >The lens of the human eye is located between the cornea and the retina. The cornea is the front of the eye whereas the retina is the layer of cells at the back of your eye. The lens of the eye is convex in shape and works with the cornea to project and focus on images directly on the retina. In turn, the retina reacts to the image, which sends signals to the brain.

>Making your prescription glasses is start by determining the prescription, or the strength of the correction needed. This takes place in the doctor’s office during your eye test. Immediately the prescription is gotten, the numerical value is sent to the laboratory to provide what is needed for the creation of the glasses. These numerical values and their meaning shouldn’t be a mystery to you because we have explained what they mean.

>Your eyeglasses start as a blank lens in the laboratory. It is either made from a glass disc or a plastic, which are about four inches wide and ½ inches thick. This blank lens has a curved front surface with a flat back surface. The lab technician takes the black lens and puts it into a machine to grind it according to the required curve. The thickness of the lens is determined by the prescription of the curve in the prescription. The grinding process needs a painstaking accuracy to ensure that the made eyeglasses are according to the prescription by the doctor. These glasses are made without any scratches on them because it may affect their function.

>After this, the lenses are edged to fit the frame chosen by the patient. At this point, the eyeglasses are coated or tinted by using a tinting solution. After this, a groove being cut is made into the lenses to ensure the glasses fit into the frame. Finally, the lenses are neatly inserted into the frame. The lenses are either treated or heated with chemicals. The chemical is important because it makes it flexible for easy insertion. Then a properly checkup is done to ensure there is no imperfection.
>Summary

>Getting your eyeglasses prescription done perfectly is crucial to your vision. Besides this, carrying out routine checkup is paramount if you value your eyes. Ensure you get your prescription from a certified doctor instead of buying “Not recommended” glasses for your eyes.

column1: 6,"Proper ways to clean, maintain, and take care of your prescription eyeglasses","Cleaning your prescription eyeglasses does not only make it look beautiful and new always, but it also help your eyes get rid of harmful bacteria, dust, and dirt which can cause eye infection and irritation. Here are some tips on how to maintain and clean your eyewear.","
column2: >Cleaning and maintaining your eyeglasses or sunglasses are just among the most effective ways of keeping your eyewear look new and beautiful. Proper cleaning does not only keep your eyewear always in good condition, it likewise helps your eyes get rid of harmful bacteria, dust, and dirt which can cause eye infection and irritation.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Eyewear"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/1.png""}}"" alt=""Eyewear"" width=""353"" height=""262"" />

column2: >There are different ways to properly clean your eyewear. But, there are also a bunch of common cleaning methods that are considered wrong and harmful for your eyeglasses or sunglasses. For instance, wiping your eyewear with the use of textured or rough fabrics can scratch its lenses.
>To make sure you don't damage your eyewear when cleaning or handling, make sure to follow any of the tips we have listed below.
>Clean your hand before holding your eyewear

>Before removing, wearing, or holding your prescription glasses, make sure that your hands are clean. The dirt and dust in your hand can possibly scratch the lenses of your eyewear and it can as well infect or irritate your eyes.
>Use both hands when holding your eyewear

>Part of taking care of your eyewear is to make sure that the lens is also in good condition. Avoid using only one hand when taking off or putting on your eyewear as this can possibly damage the frame and make it twisted, bent, or distorted. Hold each arm of the frame with the use of both your hands when putting on or taking off your eyeglasses and gently slide them over your ears.
>Keep your eyeglasses in a safe storage box

>When not in use, keep your prescription eyeglasses in their safe storage box. Don't just place them anywhere especially in areas where they can possibly bend, fall or break. Never hang them on your shirt or put them on your head as well.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""SafeBox"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/SafeBox.jpg""}}"" alt=""SafeBox"" width=""497"" height=""358"" />

column2: >If you can't find a sturdy safe box, you can use a microfiber pouch instead. This can keep your glasses away from dirt and dust.
>Wash your eyeglasses with warm water and mild dishwashing soap

>From time to time you need to give your eyewear a good quick bath to thoroughly get rid of accumulated dirt and dust. Remember not to use harmful household chemicals, cleaners or detergent when washing your eyeglasses. Use warm water and mild dishwashing soap instead.
>Wipe your eyeglasses with soft microfiber fabric

>Don't use any other type of fabric when cleaning or wiping away dirt, smears, and dust from the lenses of your glasses other than what is recommended and provided by the prescription sunglasses?manufacturer unless it is a soft microfiber cloth.

>Soft cloths and napkins seem to be safe for the lens. However, if you try to take a closer look at it, you will notice that these fabrics have small rough fabrics that can leave small scratches on the lens. The microfiber fabric is totally smooth and soft, making it safe for the lens and more effective in cleaning your eyewear.?

column1: 8,Computer glasses and their benefits,"Using a computer and staring at its screen for extended periods of time can be dangerous for the eyes. column2: >Using a computer and staring at its screen for extended periods of time can be dangerous for the eyes. In fact, around 50 to 90 percent of people who are using computers for at least 5 hours a day everyday experience eye and vision problems due to prolonged and regular use of a computer. One of the primary reasons behind this is that computer work usually has higher visual demand as compared to other activities.

column2: >Thankfully, there are different ways to alleviate computer vision syndrome and its many signs and symptoms. >What are computer glasses?

column2: >They can help reduce eye-related problems like computer vision syndrome, itchy eyes, dry eyes, red eyes, eye strain, eye twitching, and other vision-related issues associated with computer work. They help the eyes get rid of excessive irritation and tiredness at the end of each workday.
>Primary benefits of computer glasses
>Reduce glaring concern
>The light that bounces off the screen creates a glare that can obstruct proper vision. This makes it more challenging for the pupils to keep their focus and to keep up with the glaring obstacle. ?

>Computer glasses are made with an anti-reflective coating that is specifically designed to minimize the light and glare that are coming from the computer screen. They likewise come with a special glass color tinting which aids in enhancing the contrast of the screen without distorting colors and without being too raucous for the eyes.
>Encourage better posture

>You may be wondering why you need to buy glasses online when in fact you already have prescription glasses that allows you to clearly see things that are situated a few inches away from your eyes. The typical distance between the computer monitor and your face is greater than this.

>In order for you to see the screen clearly, you will have to lean forward, crane your neck, and stay in an unneutral and uncomfortable position. Doing this regularly for extended periods of time can cause extra tension and stress on the neck, shoulder and back area which can, in turn, result in poor posture and musculoskeletal pain.

column2: >Before heading to the prescription sunglasses and eyeglasses shop, make sure to know the important factors that you need to consider when buying a new pair of eyewear. Here are some of them:
>Lens Colors

>Sunglasses are available in various lens tints. The most commonly used tints are green, black, gray, brown, red, and blue. column2: >But do you know that more than just a fashion statement, each of these lens colors serves different purposes? For instance, if you intend to use the sunglasses for outdoor activities like fishing, mountain biking, cycling, skiing, and playing sports like tennis, grass field sports, water sports, and golf, you can pick a pair with green, gray, or black lens color.
>Face Shape and Frame Shape

>Prescription sunglasses are designed not only for aesthetic reasons but also for protection. The sun's UV rays are very harmful both to your skin and eyes. It has long been known to cause or trigger various vision problems like macular degeneration and some types of cataracts.

column1: 12,Picking the best frame for the shape of your face,"The shape and type of sunglasses greatly depend on the shape of your face. Unless you are not concerned about how you will look when wearing the sunglasses, it is important that you consider the shape of your face when buying a new pair of eyewear. Not only will this help you get the best fit, it will likewise help you balance your features with that of the sunglasses. ","
column2: >Once you have decided to pick the pair of sunglasses based on the primary factors that we discussed yesterday and you have already set how much you are willing to pay for your new eyewear, it is time to consider your facial features like your complexion and the shape of your face. You can further narrow down your choices if you know which types of sunglasses will best fit the shape of your face.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Rimless Eyeglasses"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Before you finally buy glasses online, it is wise to know the real shape of your face first and the most appropriate frame shape for your face. Not only will this help you get the best fit, it will likewise help you balance your features with that of the sunglasses.
>So, let's the ball rolling!
>Oval Face Shape
>If you have an oval shape face, your chin and forehead are narrower and your cheekbones are broader. People with oval face have fairly even and gently rounded features.

>If you are someone with an oval face, you can wear almost all types of frames. However, the ideal pair of prescription glasses is the one with butterfly shape frames or those frames with a top portion that is heavier, thicker, or darker lower part.
>Round Face Shape

>People with a round face has less defined angles and more obvious curves. The ideal frames for people with a round face are those that can make the face look longer, thinner, and sharper and those with fewer curves. Some good examples of these types of sunglasses are those with rectangular, wrap, square, and shield frames.
>Trapezoidal Face Shape

>A trapezoidal face is something like that of Olivia Wilde. This shape features a wider bottom part of the face. If your face is trapezoidal in shape, pick the sunglasses with dramatic cat eye frames or those with half-rim style to balance out your jawline.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Trapezoidal face"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Diamond Face Shape
>People with a diamond face has the widest cheekbone. Their jawline and forehead are also the narrowest. If you have a diamond face, pick a rimless or oval framed glasses.
>Oblong Face Shape

>An oblong face is long and narrow. It also features only a few angles. If you have an oblong or rectangular face, you will look great in oversized sunglasses. The most appropriate sunglasses styles for you are square, wayfarer, wrap, rectangular, and shield.
>Heart Face Shape
>If you have a heart-shaped face, your forehead is remarkably broader, your chin is pointed, and your jawline is narrow. For a heart-shaped face, pick rimless or aviator style sunglasses.
>Square Face Shape
>Do you have a strong jawline and remarkably broad forehead? Are the length and width of your face the same? If yes, then you have a square face.

>If you have a square face like that of Selena Gomez, choose a pair of prescription sunglasses that has rectangular or narrower oval frames with round edges. The butterfly, round, and aviator sunglasses will also look great on you.

column2: >Just like how you choose your own prescription glasses, you also need to be as detailed as possible when picking the right sunglasses for your kids. Here are a few important points that you need to take into account when buying the right quality sunglasses for your kids.

>If you think that you are at high risk of getting eye-related disorders like macular degeneration and cataracts because of the harmful effects of the sun's UV rays and you need a good pair of sunglasses to keep your eyes protected, think of how much your kids need protection from the sun as well.

column2: >As much as possible, pick the pair of kids' sunglasses that provides 99% or 100% protection against the sun's UVA and UVB rays. As early as 3 years old, you should encourage your kids to make it a habit to wear protective sunglasses whenever they need to stay outdoors.
>Durability

>Kids, in general, are naturally active and mostly they care less about the welfare of their things. When purchasing an eyewear for your kids, pay more attention to the durability than the price. If you can find very sturdy and durable glasses at a lower price, the better.

>Check if the sunglasses provide as much coverage that your child's eyes need. Make sure that the lens of the glasses does not only cover the eyes of your little one but also the skin around them.

column1: 14,6 Primary advantages of sunglasses,"The right pair of sunglasses has this ability to enhance your overall appearance. A simple outfit can further be enhanced by wearing pretty sunglasses. But more than just aesthetic reason, sunglasses offer other benefits. Let us take a look at some of the advantages that you can enjoy out of using a good pair of sunglasses.","
column2: >Wearing sunglasses is not something new in the United States and even in various parts of the world. The right pair of sunglasses has this ability to enhance your overall appearance. It can transform you into someone more attractive. By ""right pair"" I mean the eyewear that perfectly fits you and looks great on you. A simple outfit can further be enhanced by wearing pretty sunglasses. In fact, they have been considered as one of the must-have fashion accessories. But more than just aesthetic reason, sunglasses offer other benefits.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Sunglasses"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Here are a few advantages that you can enjoy out of using a good pair of sunglasses.
>Sunglasses protect your eyes from dust and dirt

>Dirt and dust are everywhere. You can never tell when they get into your eyes and when they infect them. To make sure that your eyes are protected against different kinds of dirt and dust particles, wear a pair of prescription?sunglasses most especially when you go outside or when you are in a dry area where the volume of dust and dirt in the air is quite high.
>Sunglasses protect your eyes from airborne bacteria

>Airborne bacteria can cause various types of eye-related infections like red eyes, dry eyes, irritation, and itchy eyes, among others. Typically when your eyes start to get itchy, your normal body reaction is to rub them. Rubbing the eyes with your bare hands can further exacerbate the infection most especially if your hands are dirty. Wearing sunglasses will not only prevent you from directly scratching your eyes, it will likewise keep the airborne bacteria away from your eyes in the first place.
>Sunglasses protect your eyes from the sun

>We all know that the ultraviolet rays of the sun are very harmful both to the skin and to the eyes. Direct and excessive exposure to the sun can increase your risk to different types of eye problems including some types of cataracts, pinguecula, photokeratitis, dry eye, and macular degeneration, to name a few.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Sunglasses"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >The sunglasses can protect your eyes from the harmful rays of the sun, provided that you choose a specialized pair with 99%-100% UV rays protection coating.
>Sunglasses reduce jet lag effects

>If you always travel by air and you experience jet lag everytime you have a flight, then we recommend that you wear a good pair of prescription sunglasses. Although it does not totally protect you against jet lag, wearing glasses can at least greatly reduced its effects.
>Sunglasses reduce your risk of having dark circles

>Although dark circles are primarily due to heredity and lack of rest, exposure to the sun can likewise cause dark circles. By wearing discount glasses and sunglasses, you can greatly reduce your risk of having dark circles.
>Sunglasses safeguards you against eyelid skin cancer

>The skin around your eyelids is very sensitive. Direct exposure to the sun's detrimental UV rays can increase your risk of skin cancer around the eyelid. Reports reveal that about 10% of all types of skin cancers are on the skin around the eyelids. To lower your chance of getting skin cancer, make it a habit to wear sunglasses that have UV rays protection and are large enough to cover the entire eyes and eyelid areas whenever you go outdoors.

column2: >Determining the quality of prescription glasses is quite tricky and difficult. You need to thoroughly check every part of the glasses in order for you to make sure of its quality- from the more notable parts like the frame, lens, coating,  and arms to the more intricate and smaller details like the hinges and small screws, and everything in between.

column2: >The prescription glasses are one of the most important parts of your overall wellbeing and health. They are critical especially for people who need vision correction or who need to use prescription lenses to see clearly. >With the increasing number of optical shops you can find on the market today,  the competition has also become stiffer and stiffer. With the availability of cheap glasses online, people get easily convinced to buy discount glasses without considering the quality of the product.
>Checking the quality of an eyewear

>It’s really difficult to determine the quality of an eyewear at first look but it is not impossible to know if it's weak or not. Even if you are not an expert, you can check the quality of the eyewear by doing these simple steps:

column1: • Hold both of the arms of the frames and check how the entire eyeglasses behave. Do they rattle or firm? Do they feel weak or strong? If the eyewear feels weak or if it tends to rattle, then it might not be high quality. If so,  look for something more sturdy and solid and then ditch the weak one.

• Check the quality of the hinges by trying to exercise it. Do they move smoothly? Or are they a bit stiff? Do they have the same friction all throughout the bending? If the hinges are a bit rough, if they don’t bend smoothly, or perhaps if they are too loose, then think of picking something else.

column1: 19,Different styles of fashionable sunglasses (Part 1),"People wear prescriptive sunglasses for different reasons. Some wear them for protection while others use sunglasses to jazz up their fashion style. Regardless the reason why you wear sunglasses, knowing the different styles of fashionable sunglasses available on the market today will help you choose the best pair for you.","
column2: >Sunglasses have been around for decades already. Through the years, more and more designs and styles are coming up which provide people with more options to choose from. Some of the most common types of fashionable prescription sunglasses are wrap sunglasses, round sunglasses, rectangle or square sunglasses, bowline sunglasses, aviator sunglasses, wayfarer sunglasses, retro square sunglasses, circular or round sunglasses, sports sunglasses, cat-eye sunglasses, butterfly sunglasses, shield sunglasses, clubmaster sunglasses, oversized sunglasses, and oval sunglasses.

column2: >Let us take a look at them individually.
>Oval Sunglasses

>When in doubt, opt for oval sunglasses. This type of sunglasses is considered as the safest choice for people who don't have an idea which style to pick for their face shape. The oval sunglasses can fit just about any shape of face. This basic type is indeed staple to any fashion accessory collection.
>Cat Eye Sunglasses

>The cat eye or cat eyes style is a super-cute and sexy retro style that is more popularly used for women sunglasses than men. They became famous in the 1950s and 1960s. The cat eye style sunglasses are mostly worn by women together with a Beehive hairstyle.

>The cat eyes style is perfect for those retro lovers who wish to achieve the look of Audrey Hepburn and Marilyn Monroe. It is characterized by feminine vibes, retro frames, feline prints, and upswept angles. It offers feline femininity finish with a touch of old-school charm.
>Sports Sunglasses

column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Sports Sunglasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/img_1081_1.jpg""}}"" alt=""Sports Sunglasses"" width=""760"" height=""380"" />

column2: >The sports sunglasses have lenses that are sleek and thin which typically taper at the temples. This style is specially designed for outdoor sports activities. It fights glare while enhancing visibility. The sports style sunglasses is ideal for sports enthusiasts and adventure seekers.
>Retro Square Sunglasses

>Just like the oval style sunglasses, the retro square style sunglasses is also staple to any collection. It's also a safe bet for those who are not sure what type of frame to choose. They can help balance out a round face and a narrow jawline and give them some angles. That is why they are considered as the shape-shifters. The retro square sunglasses style has a boxy shape and is often available with super-thick frames. For a more fashion-forward effect, choose those with oversized frames.
>Wayfarer Sunglasses

>The wayfarer sunglasses style is characterized with a thicker top and trapezoidal lenses. This classic style was first introduced in 1952 and has continued to grow in popularity since then. The wayfarer sunglasses style is a versatile style. It can perfectly fit almost all outfit styles and face shapes.
>Butterfly Sunglasses

>Another classic sunglasses style for women is the butterfly sunglasses. Just like the cat eye style, the butterfly style sunglasses likewise offer a uniquely pretty, feminine, and sexy effect. As its name suggests, this style ensemble the shape of a beautiful butterfly.

>This butterfly-inspired style is so fashionable and is ideal for maximum protection especially those with oversized frames. Its timeless beauty can make you feel and look like a celebrity. You can use this style all year round. It is perfect for those who want to minimize their nose arch as well.

>More styles on the second part of this post.

column1: 20,Why use prescription glasses?,"When we were in our childhood years, many of us are fond of using our grandparents' prescription glasses because we thought that they will make us look cool, smart, and geeky. But when we grew up and we needed a pair of prescription eyeglasses, we realize that it can be troublesome at times. Though there are some cons of wearing prescription glasses, they also offer some benefits. Below are some of the major advantages of prescription eyeglasses.","
column2: >When we were in our childhood years, many of us are fond of using our grandparents' prescription glasses. Some of us even tried to use eyeglasses with fake or no prescription because we thought that wearing an eyewear will make us look cool, smart, and geeky. It is only when we grew up and needed a pair of prescription eyeglasses that we realized that it's actually not as cool as what we used to think it was. Wearing eyeglasses can be troublesome at times most especially when you need to dress up for a special event and your eyewear makes it hard for you to find the best matching outfit.

column2: >Truly, there are a few disadvantages of wearing a pair of prescription glasses. However, there are also some good things that you can get out of using this type of eyewear. Below are some of the major advantages of prescription eyeglasses.
>Vision correction

>Perhaps the primary advantage and the main reason why you need to wear eyeglasses is vision correction. As we grow older, our eyes also slowly go with our age. Though this seems to be just normal, this does not mean that all people will eventually need a pair of prescription glasses when they grow older or perhaps only the aged people can wear prescription glasses. Anyone of us can be afflicted by any of the different types of eye problems that require vision correction. Wearing eyeglasses with a prescription specific to your eye needs to help you see better and clearer.
>Convenience

>Prescription glasses are more convenient and easier to use than contact lenses. Plus, it's less annoying and does not hurt the eyes. You can wear them anywhere you are and any time of the day. You just have to put them on and you are good to go. No need for you to use any eye drop or to check on them every now and then or perhaps take it out when you need to close your eyes for a couple of minutes to take a nap.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Best Glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/CA-Sunglasses5496-b_2.jpg""}}"" alt=""Best Glasses"" width=""807"" height=""380"" />

column2: >Affordability

>As compared to its close counterpart, the contact lens, the prescription glass is far more affordable. Also, prescription glasses online are more long lasting and more durable than contact lenses. Unlike contact lenses that can run out, you can use your prescription glasses multiple times or until your prescription changes provided they are still in good working condition or not broken.
>Maintenance

>Eyeglasses are easy to clean and maintain. You can use a mixture of mild dishwashing soap and lukewarm water to remove stubborn dirt or for deep cleaning and wipe dry them using a microfiber fabric. For quick cleaning, you can only just wipe away dust with the help of a soft microfiber cloth.
>Protection

>Prescription glasses keep your eyes protected from dust, dirt, and airborne bacteria that can cause irritation and infection. With your glasses on, these harmful particles can't be able to directly get through your eyes, thus, keeping them away from irritation. Also, most of the prescription eyeglasses online are made with UV protection coating that protects your eyes from the damaging rays of the sun.

column1: 21,Different styles of fashionable sunglasses (Part 2),"Prescription sunglasses are available in many different styles. Each of these styles offers a distinctive beauty and effect. column2: >So, here's the continuation of the different types and styles of sunglasses available right now. Check out which of these styles fit you well and get ready for a more uniquely stunning go-to look.

column2: >Shield Sunglasses

>The shield sunglasses style gives you the most generous coverage. It covers your entire eyes and the surrounding sensitive skin from front to side and protects them from the harmful rays of the sun. So if you are planning to stay outdoors during a hot sunny day for extended periods of time, it is best to pick the shield style sunglasses.
>Browline Sunglasses

>The browline sunglasses style is designed with an upper frame that is super thick. As its name suggests, this style's upper frame run across the brow. It was first introduced in 1947 but was not that widely known until after the clubmaster style was made popular.
>Clubmaster Sunglasses

>The clubmaster style sunglasses is almost similar to the browline style sunglasses. It also has a traditional browline frame that is substantially thicker than the bottom frame. The clubmaster sunglasses style is typically made of plastic or acetate. It also offers a classic look.
>Rectangle Sunglasses

>The rectangle or rectangular sunglasses style comes with frames that have greater width measurement than their height. This style has wide and rectangular lenses, hence the name rectangle sunglasses.

column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Rectangle Sunglasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Rectangle_Sunglasses.png""}}"" alt=""Rectangle Sunglasses"" width=""760"" height=""380"" />

column2: >This simple yet elegant wide sunglasses style is perfect for those with round faces. If you are looking for something that is not too fancy, this awesome style is also worth a try. You will find rectangle sunglasses with a variety of edges that range from more rounded to angular.
>Wrap Style Sunglasses

>For a swept back look, your best option is the wrap style sunglasses. This type of sunglasses provides maximum sun protection, making it ideal for athletes, and for people with an active lifestyle who are always on the go. It is also great for outdoor sports and driving.
>Round Sunglasses

>When you hear something about round style sunglasses, what comes first to your mind aside from its shape? I guess you will think of the legendary John Lennon as well. This iconic hipster counterculture style has circular shape lenses. It comes in various lens size, frame colors, materials, and frame thickness. The most popular type of this style is the Teashades, also popularly referred to as John Lennon glasses, which is known for its wire-rim glasses.

>The round prescription sunglasses style offers an eccentric, and distinctive look. It is best worn by people with strong, angular, or edgy facial features. Be careful in picking this style, though, as not all people can successfully pull it off.
>Aviator Sunglasses

column2: >The last but definitely not the least in this list is the aviator style sunglasses. This is perhaps the most favorite and the most famous sunglasses style available today. The aviator sunglasses are first introduced in 1936 and were initially designed for pilots. This style is characterized by its unique teardrop shape. With its simple yet classic style, you can never go wrong with a pair of aviator style sunglasses.

>So, that wraps up all the different styles of sunglasses for both men and women available on the market today.

column1: 26,Different types of prescriptions lenses available at California Glasses,"If you need a new pair of prescription glasses but you are not sure which type of prescription lens to choose, this post will definitely help you. Today we will discuss the 4 different types of prescription lenses that we offer here at California Glasses.","
column2: >If you need a new pair of prescription glasses but you are not sure which type of prescription lens to choose, this post will definitely help you. Today we will discuss the 4 different types of prescription lenses that we offer here at California Glasses.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""California Glasses"" alt=""California Glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/California_Glasses_frames.png""}}"" />

column2: >Reading Glasses

>The reading glasses are actually not a prescription lens. As their name suggests, reading glasses are something that you don't wear all the time. They are only typically used when reading or when you need to enlarge the thing that you want to see in front of your eyes.

>Reading glasses are like magnifying lenses that make it easier for your eyes to see and focus on small objects like during reading books or similar thing that have small text. They do not merely help your read comfortably and see the text clearly, they likewise help you get rid of eye strain.
>Single Vision Glasses

>As their name suggests, the single vision glasses feature only one optical power over the entire lenses. They are typically used for correcting one field of vision. For instance, if you only need to clearly see things up close like when you are reading, at an intermediate distance like when using a computer, or at a long distance like when driving, you can use a pair of single vision glasses.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""single vision glasses"" alt=""single vision glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/single_vision_glasses.png""}}"" />

column2: >Progressive Glasses

>If you need to wear glasses for vision correction but you don't want to look too old, progressive glasses are ideal for you. Also known as no-line bifocals, progressive lenses do not have any traditional lines visible on traditional lenses like bifocals and trifocals.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Progressive Glasses"" alt=""Progressive Glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Progressive_Glasses.png""}}"" />

column2: >Progressive glasses are considered the most fashionable prescription lenses. Progressive glasses allow you to continue to clearly see everything that's in front of your eyes at all distances.
>If you are wearing these multifocal lenses, people won't know that you are using prescription glasses for vision correction. They will just think that you are using them as a fashion accessory.
>Bifocal Glasses

>If you have presbyopia and you need glasses not only to correct such eye problem but also some other vision issues like astigmatism, myopia, and hyperopia, you might already have come across bifocal glasses before. But for those unfamiliar yet about this type of eyewear, bifocal lenses are prescription glasses with two different optical powers.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Bifocal Glasses"" alt=""Bifocal Glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Bifocal_Glasses.png""}}"" />

column2: >Bifocal glasses typically offer a wider lens area for computer work and reading as compared to progressive glasses. That is why despite its not-so-good- visual appeal, many people especially the older ones still prefer it over the other types of lenses for prescription glasses.

>Generally, the lower portion of the bifocal lenses offers near vision correction. It makes it less challenging for your eyes to stay in focus and enables you to see things in the short distance very clearly. On the other end, the larger part of the prescription lenses is intended for distant vision correction.

column1: 27,California Glasses| Primary benefits of prescription sunglasses,"We wear sunglasses for fashion. It's definitely hard to stay fashionable with prescription eyeglasses. column2: >A few decades ago, people wear sunglasses only for fashion. But today, not anymore. More than being a piece of fashion accessory, we now wear sunglasses primarily for protection. column2: >To protect our skin from the harmful rays of the sun, we typically apply sunblock or any lotion or body cream with SPF and use an umbrella or wear a hat whenever we need to stay outdoors during hot sunny days. But just like our skin, our eyes also need protection from the sun. And one way to protect our eyes is to wear a pair of sunglasses with 100% UV protection.

>We have previously talked about the different advantages of wearing sunglasses. Apart from protection from the sun's damaging UV rays, sunglasses also keep our eyes safe from various airborne bacteria, dust, grime, and other small particles present in the air that can cause eye irritation or infection. It also protects our eyes from eyelid skin cancer. Sunglasses help reduce risks of having dark circles and minimize the effects of jet lag.

>But how about the prescription sunglasses? Do they also provide the same advantages as the regular sunglasses? You may ask. On top of the primary benefits regular sunglasses provide, the prescription sunglasses offer a few additional advantages. Here's a few of them.
>Protection from different eye problems

>Prescription sunglasses also protect your eyes from various eye and vision problems including but limited to dry eyes, cataracts, macular degeneration, photokeratitis or sunburn of the eye, and Pinguecula or the eye condition that is characterized by eye growth formation on the on the white portion of the eye.

column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Prescription sunglasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Prescription_sunglasses.png""}}"" alt=""Prescription sunglasses"" />

column2: >Eye protection and vision improvement

>As stated above, sunglasses provide eye protection. High-quality prescription sunglasses like the ones offered at California Glasses do not only have 100% UV protection, they also include glare protection to make it more relaxing and easier for the eyes to see even when there are unwanted scattered lights or the sun is too bright which can possibly blur your vision. A prescription is also added for vision correction, making it possible for you to see very clearly even when wearing tinted glasses.

>Typically, when you wear ordinary sunglasses and your eyes require prescription lenses for vision correction, you have to wear contact lenses. It's definitely hard to stay fashionable with prescription eyeglasses. You need to choose between protecting your eyes from the sunlight by wearing sunglasses but you will not be able to see clearly and prioritizing vision correction over eye protection. With prescription sunglasses, you do not have to choose between these two options as prescription sunglasses offer both eye protection and vision improvement.

column1: 30,Different materials for eyeglasses frames,"You would like to know the different frame material options for your new glasses, right? Frames can be made of different materials but the most common are metal, bendable glasses, and plastic. Learn more about this different materials here.","
column2: >One of the major things that you need to think of when shopping for prescription glasses is the material used for the frame you have chosen. You would like to know the different frame material options for your new glasses, right? If so, then check the frame materials listed below.

column2: >Frames can be made of different materials but the most common are metal, bendable glasses, and plastic.
>Metal Frames

>Mostly used for prescription glasses, metal frames are can be made from different types of metal elements. This include but is not limited to titanium, beta-titanium, beryllium, memory metal, monel, aluminum, and stainless steel.
>Titanium frames

>One other widely used metal for manufacturing eyewear frames is titanium. This metal is known for its great characteristics. Many people prefer titanium frames for their eyeglasses for some reasons. Aside from being corrosion-resistant, it is likewise very resilient, sturdy, strong and hypoallergenic.
>Titanium is likewise very light making it ideal for those who wear eyeglasses all the time. Its weight is approximately 40% lesser than the other available metals used for eyeglasses.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Titanium Frames"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Eyeglasses.png""}}"" alt=""Titanium Frames"" />

column2: >Stainless steel frames

>The stainless steel frames are known for being strong, lightweight, flexible, and naturally corrosion-resistant. They also have a low toxicity level and are nickel-free. Stainless steel is of chromium and steel.

>They are popular for their polished, glossy finish which makes the glasses look more beautiful. You can also find stainless steel frames with a matte finish. The stainless steel frames are a great alternative to the more expensive but equally sturdy titanium frames. They are reasonably priced, making it the best choice for people who are looking for strong and beautifully designed yet highly affordable frames.
>Plastic Frames

>There are different kinds of materials used for manufacturing plastic frames. However, the most commonly used for prescription glasses frames are zylonate or Zyl, propionate, nylon, and cellulose acetate. Plastic frames offer many advantages over some metal materials. Plastic frames tend to be more fashionable and affordable. However, they are also less durable than metal frames. They can easily break and can fade over time most especially when always exposed to direct sunlight.
>Propionate is a nylon-based plastic that is commonly used in sports glasses. It is very durable, hypoallergenic, flexible, and lightweight.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Plastic Frames"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Plastic_Frames.png""}}"" alt=""Plastic Frames"" width=""458"" height=""308"" />

column2: >Cellulose acetate is a plant-based plastic that is used to create a flexible, lightweight, strong, and hypoallergenic eyeglasses frame. ?It is more durable than nylon.

>Zylonite, or simply Zyl, is perhaps the most popular plastic eyewear frame. It is readily available, relatively light, and very inexpensive as well. Zyl frames are available in different colors. You can even find Zyl frames with two or more colors within one frame. If you are looking for fashionable prescription eyeglasses online that you can use to match any outfit, Zyl frames are the best bet.

>Consider buying more than one pair so you will have something different to wear for every outfit, look, and occasion.

column1: 31,Debunking reading and prescription eyeglasses myths,"When we were younger, our grandparents and even our parents tell us many believable statements about eye health and wearing reading and prescription glasses. Because we trust their words, we believed them and followed their advice. Only when we grew matured when we realized that not all of them are true. Let us take a look at some of the common statements that we once believe are true but are actually just myths.","
column2: >When we were younger, our grandparents and even our parents always tell us not to wash our face right after getting up from the bed as doing so will only harm our eyesight. When I grew older and got vision problem, I was told to eat as many carrots as possible (from fresh carrot sticks to carrot cakes and every carrot recipe you can ever think of) to restore my eye health. My eye doctor had discovered that I have a vision problem when I was still ten or twelve. I was advised to wear prescription eyeglasses but my parents did not follow what the doctor said as they believe that it can only worsen my condition.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Eyeglasses myths"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Facts.png""}}"" alt=""Eyeglasses myths"" width=""458"" height=""308"" />

column2: >Are these scenarios familiar with you? Are these things really true or are they just myths? Let us take a look at some of the common statements that we once believe are true but are actually just myths.
>Myth #1 - Eyeglasses can impair your vision over time

>As we grow older, our eyes can also get weary and old. And this can have some effects on our vision. Prescription eyeglasses are designed to help improve your vision to allow you to see clearly just like when you still have no eye problem. They don't worsen your vision problem or create another eye illness in any way.
>Myth #2- OTC reading glasses are bad for your eyes

>If over the counter reading glasses are bad for your eyes, why would pharmacies sell them in the first place? Pharmacies know what is good or bad for you and they won't surely do something that can harm you or worsen your eye problem.

>However, before you buy OTC reading glasses, make sure that you know exactly what magnification lens best fits your needs. Using a pair of reading glasses with higher or lower magnification than your eyes need can be annoying and can cause discomfort but rest assured, it can't damage your vision. If possible have your eyes checked by a licensed eye doctor before buying and using non-prescription OTC reading glasses.
>Myth #3 - Wearing prescription glasses will make you look like a nerd
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""prescription glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Wearing_prescription_glasses.png""}}"" alt=""prescription glasses"" width=""458"" height=""308"" />

column2: >If we are talking about this a few decades ago, then it can be partly true. However, in this modern generation, you can find almost countless of prescription glasses that have chic designs. California Glasses, for instance, offers prescription glasses with pretty designs, modern styles, and chic shades which provide you vision correction while allowing you to look fabulous.
>Myth #4 - The best way to treat eye problem is to eat a lot of carrots and not to wear prescription eyeglasses

>Many people believe that prescription glasses can further exacerbate eye problems. The truth is, it can neither treat the eye problem nor make it worse. Prescription glasses are only intended to correct vision problems and not to cure it.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Vision Glasses"" src=""{{media url=""wysiwyg/Computer_Glasses_1.jpg""}}"" alt=""Vision Glasses"" width=""458"" height=""308"" />

column2:
>On the part of eating a lot of carrots, there are still no studies that testify to its effectiveness in curing vision problems. Carrots contain an abundant amount of vitamins and minerals that can help make the eyes healthier and help minimize the impacts of eye problems like age-related macular degeneration and cataracts. They can't, however, cure vision problems.

column1: Wearing eyeglasses all the time can be both burdensome and annoying sometimes. But worry no more because we have here some tips and tricks that you can use to avoid or at least minimize such burden.

column2: >Buy microfiber cloth in bulk

>Buying just one or two microfiber fabrics can be a bit expensive. How about buying in bulk at once? You can save more when you buy in wholesale. You can also buy the larger one instead of just the minis. You can always cut it into smaller pieces if you want to.
>Pick the right optical shop

>Prescription glasses do not have to be always expensive. There is quite a lot of less known companies out there that offer equally amazing and high-quality cheap glasses at a relatively lower price. In most cases, you are paying only for the brand name and not actually the quality of glasses if you choose to opt for expensive ones.
margin-left: auto
column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Wearing eyeglasses"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Stay away from harsh cleaning chemicals

>One of the problems of prescription glasses online is the protective coating. However, it does not always have to do with the quality of the lens but to how you care for your glasses. Cleaning chemicals and substances that contain bleach, alcohol, vinegar, and ammonia can damage the lenses' coating. So, avoid them as much as possible. Use diluted dish soap instead when washing your eyewear.
>Adjusting nose pads the right way

>It's sometimes quite challenging to balance the nose pads. A quick trick to this is to place your frames on a flat surface and then adjust the nose pads. This way you can easily see if they're balanced or not.
>Finding your glasses on dark areas

>Are you struggling in locating your glasses or their casing when you are in a dark area? How about doing this trick- attach a neon colored or glow in the dark paint, strip, or sticker on any part of your prescription glasses (preferably on the tip of the frame) and on the casing as well. This way you can easily see where your glasses and their casing are located in case you lost them in the dark.

column3: margin-right: auto
column4: title=""Prescription glasses"" src=""{{media url=&quot

column2: >Bring with you a microfiber cloth anywhere you are

>Your glasses can get misty or cloudy a few times a day especially when you are out in a dusty place, when it's raining, or when the weather is quite misty. It's smart to bring with you a clean microfiber cloth always anywhere you are.

>If you are changing your bag or pouch often, it helps that you get some microfiber cloths ready and put one in each of your bags to make sure you won't forget it. Chances are when you don't have a microfiber cloth on hand, you can't clean your glasses right away when you need to. Or worse, you may be tempted to wipe them with any fabric you have on hand which can possibly damage or scratch the lenses.

>So there you go, some tips and tricks to make using prescription glasses more pleasing for you.

column2: >Safety glasses are for the people who put their eyes at risk while working. These people are at high risk of eye injury, and so they need to wear safety glasses at their workplace. >If the lenses are severely scratched, then opt for replacement as such lenses are at high risk of crack and break.

column2: >You should know how to perform the cleaning of your safety glasses. Use water or recommend cleansing solution by the manufacturer to clean the lenses.

column1: Microfiber Bag is also an excellent option to clean the lenses:

column1: 42,How Do Safety Glasses and Goggles Differ from Regular Eyeglasses?,Regular eyeglasses and safety glasses have different functionalities. The primary function of regular eyeglasses or prescription eyeglasses is to correct your vision problem. Safety glasses are designed to protect your eyes when you are performing special tasks that pose a higher risk of eye injury.,"

Regular eyeglasses and safety glasses have different functionalities. The primary function of regular eyeglasses or prescription eyeglasses is to correct your vision problem. Safety glasses are designed to protect your eyes when you are performing special tasks that pose a higher risk of eye injury.

If you are working in a facility or a site, you should invest in a pair of safety glasses to give complete protection to your eyes. Workers wearing regular eyeglasses or goggles at the job sites are at higher risk of eye injuries as their eyes are not getting sufficient protection against flying wood chips and other dangerous debris.
You can rely on standard prescription glasses or sunglasses to protect your eyes against UV rays, but, as they have minimum impact resistance, they are not ideal for an industrial environment.?

Workers who are prescribed to wear glasses should opt for prescription safety glasses rather than wearing regular glasses or wearing two pairs at once. Prescription safety glasses eliminate the need for choosing between clear vision and eye protection. It should be clear that either worker should opt for safety glasses or prescription safety glasses or goggles while performing special tasks.

Prescription glasses usually cost more than regular lenses. Employers can approach CA Glasses to avail their workers high quality and affordable prescription safety glasses and provide perfect eye protection to the employees.
How Safety Glasses Protect the Eyes Better than Regular Eyeglasses?

Safety glasses are highly impact-resistant than regular eyewear, and it goes through several tests ensuring the maximum protection for eyes. Just like prescription safety glasses, you can also find prescription safety goggles to work comfortably outdoors.

ANSI (American National Standards Institute) rates the safety glasses. Safety glasses usually have a stamp on the right side of the arm of the frame. It can be Z87 or Z87+, and the plus rating indicates the high impact resistance. The stamp is the assurance that both lens and frame are created carefully to meet specific impact resistant. Regular eyewear does not have such specifications and is not recommended to wear while working on sites.
How Ratings are Given to the Safety Glasses:

column2: >Z87:

column1: For the lens to achieve Z87 rating, it has to go through a drop ball test. A still ball of one-inch diameter is dropped on the lens for the height of 50 inches. In case of cracking, chipping, or breaking, the lens fails to achieve Z87 standard.

column2: >Z87+:

>The high impact test involves shooting .25 inch steel ball at the lens at the speed of 150 feet per second. If the lens does not crack, break, or chip, then it is rated as high impact resistant. The lens is also required to hold its position without dislodging from the frame.

column1: When it comes to regular glasses and sunglasses, they do not go through such intense tests for impact resistant. The lenses are tested only for quality and protection against UV rays. The purpose of prescription glasses to provide vision clarity with the comfortable fitting of stylish frames. Standard eyewear is not designed to protect eyes from ash, sparks, flying debris, or sawdust.
OSHA does not Consider Wearing Regular Glasses at Workplace Safe:

OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) does not consider wearing regular glasses or sunglasses at work reliable. Employers are recommended to enforce safety glasses standards at the workplace. If the standards are not maintained properly, then citation can be issued against the employers.

You can wear sunglasses or regular prescription glasses to have a clear vision and style when you walk out. But that accessory should not be considered protective for eyes while doing specialized tasks. Those who don't work in an industrial environment should keep a pair handy when working on a home renovation project.

Safety glasses and goggles are different from regular glasses. Safety glasses are high impact resistant and keep your eyes safe at the workplace, while regular glasses and sunglasses serve the purpose of correcting vision and look stylish at the same time. column1: 44,5 Tips for Workplace Eye Safety,"Eye injuries are common in the workplace. It is important to keep the eyes well-protected while working in an environment where particles, sparks, chemicals, dust, or chips pose a threat to eye safety. ","

column2: >Eye injuries are common in the workplace. It is important to keep the eyes well-protected while working in an environment where particles, sparks, chemicals, dust, or chips pose a threat to eye safety. Eye injury may leave employee spending days on a bed for recovery. Employees spending days without working is also counterproductive for the business. It is in the best interest of everyone to prioritize eye safety at the workplace. Worksites, where the risk of eye injury is high, has to follow a certain guideline to protect eyes against that specific working environment of the place. Chemical factories, construction sites, timber cutting sites, etc. Should have strict rules to reduce the risk of eye injuries. Here are some useful eye safety tips.

>Wearing safety glasses suited for your work condition is essential to protect eyes. If dust, flying particles, sparks, etc. are a frequent hazard at the site then you should invest in high-quality safety glasses. Safety glasses are available in different variations and you should buy a pair which is comfortable wearing and suited for your worksite. For example, if you need protection from sides, you should purchase glasses with side-shields for extra protection. If you work outdoors then wear safety sunglasses to get protection against harmful UV rays. If you are prescribed to wear glasses then you should opt for prescription safety sunglasses rather than being uncomfortable wearing two glasses at once while performing tasks. Construction site workers are highly recommended to wear safety sunglasses as they have to protect their eyes from dust and particles as well as harsh UV rays.

>Safety glasses are not only for keeping the eyes protected all the time while working, but it is also utilized for clear vision. It is difficult for any worker to perform tasks with precision when the vision is not clear as it should be due to scratches or dust on the lenses. Whether you are wearing prescription safety glasses or non-prescribed safety glasses, cleaning the lenses using the right material keeps your vision clear while working. Clear lenses enhance safety and also improve the quality of work as it gets done with the required precision. While purchasing the glasses, check if the lenses are scratch-resistant or not. If you work outdoors, ensure that the frame, as well as lenses, are manufactured considering several factors, such as fog, sweat, and harsh sunlight. For outdoor working, you should opt for quality polarized safety sunglasses. Polarized glasses allow only vertical lights to pass through the lens and minimize the blinding effect caused by glare. Wearing safety glasses with a polarized lens gives you a clear, unobstructed view.

column1: Reduce Hazards as much as Possible at the Worksite:

column2: >Reducing hazards at the workplace is an organic process that can be achieved by implementing several rules and regulations and developing a few habits to improve the level of eye safety. Educating and training workers about the present risk and encouraging general safety is helpful. A workplace should have a protocol that encourages general safety among workers. Workers should also get proper training under an experienced supervisor for educating them about workplace safety.

>Employees should work using the best safety equipment, but at the same time having prepared for minor to major injuries is also crucial. Having first aid kit handy is necessary. It is useful in treating work-related minor injuries. You should also have eyewash readily available to address minor irritation caused by dust or grit. If possible, a workplace with a hazardous working environment should have an emergency eyewash station to take care of more serious eye injuries caused by exposure to acids or chemicals.

>At the workplace, safety glasses are used on a daily basis and if they are not placed properly after use then it can be damaged. Scratches and cracks on the lenses reduce visibility and fail to give comprehensive protection. To keep the glasses safe, it should be cleaned regularly and placed at a safe place. With a little maintenance care, your safety glasses will remain in perfect condition for a long time.

column1: 48,What are the Common Causes of Workplace Eye Injuries?,"Doctors across the United States attend to thousands of eye injury cases. Many of these injuries are caused by workplace accidents. It is noted that many of these injuries were preventable with wearing safety glasses as most of the injuries caused by flying debris and exposure to harmful chemicals, or fumes, and also from excessive strain to the eyes. Let?s see what are the most common causes of workplace eye injuries.","
column2: wysiwyg/eyes_injury.jpg&quot

column1: Doctors across the United States attend to thousands of eye injury cases. Many of these injuries are caused by workplace accidents. It is noted that many of these injuries were preventable with wearing safety glasses as most of the injuries caused by flying debris and exposure to harmful chemicals, or fumes, and also from excessive strain to the eyes. Let?s see what are the most common causes of workplace eye injuries.

Flying debris is one of the most common hazards at the workplace that involve engineering, demolition, manufacturing, building construction, etc. Employees are required to wear safety glasses to prevent damage from flying debris as it can cause minor to severe eye damage. Even a small piece of derbies can cause irreparable eye damage. Even a small piece of grit can scratch the cornea and cause permanent vision loss.

Radiation is harmful to human bodies if it exceeds its safety limits. Employees who have to work outdoors every day for long hours should wear protective eyewear to reduce the risk of eye injury. Exposure to UV radiation increases the risk of several eye-related conditions, such as itching, dryness, reddening, excessive watering, and carcinomas of various parts of the eye. If you work outdoors for extended hours regularly then you should invest in a pair of quality safety sunglasses.

There are several tools used in different types of workplaces that can cause a variety of eye-related issues. For example, employees working in the office in using a computer for hours are at greater risk of developing dry eyes, altered vision, and pain.

Employees working in biology labs frequently use radiation lamps to kill bacteria and germs. If their eyes remain exposed to radiation for certain hours then it can cause minor to extensive eye damage.

Welding is the task where the welder has to be extra careful to protect his eyes. Emission of UV rays during the welding process can damage the eyes. The constant brightness of flash during welding can also cause pain.

When corrosive chemicals come in contact with eyes, it can cause extensive damage to the eyes. Eyes can be significantly damaged even if only the fumes of the chemical comes in contact with eyes. It can cause, slight burning, watery eyes, and can also lead to permanent vision loss.

If any object hits the eyes with sufficient force, it can badly damage the eyes. At the workplace where there is no shortage of pointed or blunt objects, the eyes are required to be protected all the time.

This is also a common cause of eye injuries at the workplace. When a worker has weak eyesight, he or she should wear prescription safety glasses to protect their eyes and working without compromising with their vision. Even a small miscalculation can be life-threatening not only for the employee with weak eyesight but also for other employees working around him.
How to reduce eye injuries at the workplace:

column2: wysiwyg/eyes_safety.jpg&quot

column1: Most of the eye injuries at the workplace are the result of negligence. If proper safety protocols are implemented at the workplace then the risk of eye injury can be significantly reduced. By taking the following precautions, workers and employers can create a working environment that minimizes the risk of eye injury.

Hazard assessments should be performed regularly to reduce the possibility of an accident occurring in the workplace. Ensuring that all equipment is in perfect condition and other items such as machine guards and boiler covers are placed properly helps minimize the risk of workplace accidents.

The employer should provide quality protective eyewear and other safety equipment to the workers. An employer can collaborate with a company that provides best safety glasses, safety sunglasses, and prescription safety glasses.
Proper training should be given to the workers. Proper knowledge about the potential risk should be given to them.

A first aid kit should be placed where it can be easily reached in case of an emergency. All workplaces should also have saline eyewash and availability of clean water to minimize the damage when an accident occurs.

Prevention is better than cure. Workers must take all the necessary precautions to protect their eyes. From wearing high-quality safety glasses to being educated about the potential risks at the workplace help workers to perform daily tasks with minimum risk of injuries. 49,How to identify prescription safety glasses Z87.1?,"Eye protection is something that should be worn by workers in almost every industry. Employers, particularly those in fields where there is the possibility of hazardous materials being present, should make eye safety their top priority in order to protect their employees. Workers in the construction industry can protect themselves from sawdust, workers in laboratories can protect themselves from harmful chemical splashes, and workers in the metalworking industry can protect themselves from sparks, shrapnel, and other hazards.","
column2: wysiwyg/shutterstock_1261496170_1__1.jpg&quot
column3: }}"" alt=""safety glasses"" />

column2: >Eye protection is something that should be worn by workers in almost every industry. Employers, particularly those in fields where there is the possibility of hazardous materials being present, should make eye safety their top priority in order to protect their employees. Workers in the construction industry can protect themselves from sawdust, workers in laboratories can protect themselves from harmful chemical splashes, and workers in the metalworking industry can protect themselves from sparks, shrapnel, and other hazards.

>Therefore, it is essential to ensure that the appropriate eye protection is worn at all times. By placing an emphasis on eye safety, the American National Standards Institute (ANSI) hopes to facilitate a process that is both simpler and more secure. Its standard for eye protection, ANSI Z87.1, lays out the requirements for utilizing, testing, marking, selecting, and maintaining eye protection in order to prevent or reduce injuries caused by eye hazards.
>This article provides a breakdown of the standard in order to assist you in making an educated decision when selecting ANSI-compliant safety glasses.?
>Indications of prescription safety glasses Z87.1

>Eye protection must bear markings in accordance with ANSI Z87.1, and those markings must relate directly to the device's capacity to defend against particular hazards. Eye protection glasses that comply with Z87.1 will have the letters ""Z87"" printed on them.
>Additional markings can be broken down into the following categories:

>The ANSI Z87.1 standard divides eye protection into two categories: impact-rated and non-impact-rated. Eye protection with an impact rating needs to be able to withstand certain high-mass and high-velocity tests, in addition to offering protection to the wearer's eyes from the side. Eye protection that is rated for impact will have a plus sign (+) on it. Flat lenses with an impact rating, for instance, will have the marking ""Z87+"" on them.
>Protection against Dust and Chemical Splashes
>Eyewear that complies with the requirements of ANSI Z87.1 for protection against droplets (splashes) or dust will be marked with a code that starts with the letter ""D."" Take, for instance:

column1: The letter ""D3"" is printed on eye protection that shields the wearer's eyes from both droplets and splashes. Workers who deal with chemicals on daily basis can go for it.
Eye protection that also protects against dust is designated with the ""D4"" label. Workers like carpenters can wear these.
The designation ""D5"" refers to eye protection that offers protection against fine dust. These are ideal for factory workers who operate big machienaries.

column2: >Protection Against Optical Radiation

>A letter designation, which is typically followed by a rating number, denotes the level of protection against radiation that the lenses of a pair of safety glasses can provide. These are the markings that have been made:

column1: The welding filter is denoted by the letter ""W"" followed by a shade number ranging from 1.3 to 14.
Filter for ultra-violet rays, denoted by the letter ""U"" followed by a number between 2 and 6.
Filter for infrared radiation (heat): ""R"" followed by a number on a scale ranging from 1.3 to 10.
""L"" followed by a number on a scale ranging from ""1.3"" to ""10"" denotes the Visible Light (Glare) Filter.
Clear lens: There is no further marking

column2: >When necessary, eye protection will also feature two additional markings, which are as follows:

column1: Eye protection that is sized appropriately for a person with a smaller head will be denoted with the letter ""H.""
Prescription safety glasses that OSHA has approved will have the manufacturer's logo on them.

column2: >Eye protection glasses can have multiple markings on them if they satisfy the criteria for two or more categories at the same time. For instance, lenses marked ""Z87+L8D3D4"" provide impacted-rated eye protection, as well as glare reduction, protection against droplets and dust, and protection against other types of debris. You can choose the type of safety and protection you need for your eyes based on your profession or the day-to-day activities that you perform.
